title: Light Fried Japanese Onion Skewers
description: A kid-friendly Japanese dish featuring lightly fried onion rings, marinated in a sweet and savory sauce. This keto-friendly recipe uses fermented ingredients for added depth of flavor.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice vinegar, coconut aminos, sesame oil, grated ginger, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the sliced onion rings to the marinade and toss to coat. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix together almond flour and a pinch of salt.
Dip each marinated onion ring into the almond flour mixture, coating lightly, then into the beaten eggs, making sure they are fully coated.
Heat about 1-2 inches of sesame oil in a large skillet over medium heat until it reaches 350°F.
Fry the onion rings in batches until they are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the fried onion rings from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Thread 2-3 fried onion rings onto each skewer.
For an extra crispy coating, you can chill the coated onion rings in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before frying.
